narrative_summary: The BRCA1 missense variant E1214K (c.3640G>A or c.3655G>A) alters the carboxy‑terminal region of the protein and has been evaluated in the context of hereditary breast and ovarian cancer susceptibility. Clinical data from a Norwegian cohort showed that the variant was initially classified as a variant of uncertain significance (class 3) but, after inter‑laboratory data sharing, was re‑classified to a likely benign status (class 2) [PMC9636114]. Computational analyses, however, have highlighted a potential functional impact. One study predicted that the substitution abolishes binding of casein kinase II (CK2) to the BRCA1 region surrounding serine 1214, a change interpreted as damaging and suggestive of pathogenicity [PMC3660339]. A separate investigation of early‑stage high‑grade serous ovarian cancers identified the same allele (rs80356923) as having predicted deleterious effects on homologous recombination repair, again supporting a pathogenic interpretation [PMC4538798]. The clinical re‑classification to likely benign conflicts with the in silico predictions of a disruptive effect on kinase interaction and DNA repair pathways, indicating uncertainty in the variant’s true impact. While the most recent clinical assessment favors a benign or likely benign interpretation, the computational evidence raises the possibility of functional relevance that has not yet been confirmed experimentally. Consequently, the current evidence base is discordant, and the pathogenicity of BRCA1 E1214K remains uncertain, warranting further functional validation and population‑level data before a definitive classification can be assigned.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study surveys BRCA1 variant classifications in Norway, highlighting that the E1214K (c.3640G>A) variant was re‑classified from a VUS (class 3) to likely benign (class 2) after data sharing between laboratories.
article_mentions.title: BRCA1 Norway: comparison of classification for BRCA1 germline variants detected in families with suspected hereditary breast and ovarian cancer between different laboratories

mentions.quoted_text: ...(C0) Phosphorylation site occupancy during Mitosis BRCA1 p.G1201S c.3601G>A rs55725337 11D 3 S1201 creates NEK2, PRKCD, PRKCI, PRKCQ, PRKCZ, PRKCA, PRKCG binding at HLAQSYRRGA Benign (C0) BRCA1 **p.E1214K** c.3655G>A N/A 11D 9 K1214 abolishes CK2A1 and CSNK2A1 binding to S1211* in AKKLESSEEN and S1212 in KKLESSEENL Damaging (C0) BRCA1 p.S1217P c.3649T>C N/A 11D 1 P1217 abolishes CK2A1 and CSNK2A1...

Casein Kinase II (CK2) is a ubiquitous protein serine/threonine kinase involved in SSB repair of chromosomal DNA. It was first described to bind and phosphorylate the carboxyl region of BRCA1 (amino acids between 1345-1863) at Ser1572 . In cell cycle regulation it is required in the transition from G0 to G1 and G1 to S. NetworKIN prediction showed that the predicted kinase for the biologically uncharacterized sites Ser403, Ser454, Ser749, Ser1214, Ser1217, Ser1218, and Ser1577 to be CK2 and CSNK2A1. In support of the functional significance of this observation, four of the five BRCA1 VUS (S454N, S1217P, S1218C and S1577P) which directly mutated serine residues at Ser454, Ser1217, Ser1218, and Ser1577 are predicted to abrogate CK2/CSNK2A1 binding to these sites. In fact 35% (7/20) BRCA1 VUS (S403F, S454N, D749Y, **E1214K**, S1217P, S1218C and S1577P) are predicted to result in the abrogation of CK2A1 and CSNK2A1 interaction on these sites while N417S and P1502S created a binding site for these two kinases at Ser417 and Ser1502, respectively.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study predicts that the BRCA1 E1214K missense variant disrupts CK2 kinase binding, suggesting it may be pathogenic despite being classified as a VUS.
article_mentions.title: Missense Variants of Uncertain Significance (VUS) Altering the Phosphorylation Patterns of BRCA1 and BRCA2

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study of 16 low‑stage high‑grade serous ovarian cancers finds TP53 alterations as the earliest events, with BRCA1/2 mutations and homologous‑recombination repair defects occurring in a subset of tumors, indicating early involvement of these pathways in tumorigenesis.
article_mentions.title: TP53 mutations, tetraploidy and homologous recombination repair defects in early stage high-grade serous ovarian cancer
